message:
feat(roster): add agent roster schema and TypeScript implementation

Implements brenner_bot-5so.12.1.1: Define agent roster schema + config contract

- Add specs/agent_roster_schema_v0.1.md with:
  - Core types (RosterEntry, Roster, RosterPreset)
  - Edge case rules (no duplicate agents, duplicate roles allowed)
  - Loading precedence (CLI > config > defaults)
  - Serialization format for kickoff messages

- Add apps/web/src/lib/roster.ts with:
  - TypeScript types matching the spec
  - Validation functions (validateRoster, validateRosterCoverage)
  - Utility functions (getRosterEntry, getAgentsByRole)
  - Serialization (formatRosterAsMarkdown, parseRosterJson)
  - Built-in presets (default-3-agent, hypothesis-critique)

- Add 26 comprehensive tests in roster.test.ts

This enables explicit role assignment for real Agent Mail identities
(e.g., BlueLake, PurpleMountain) instead of heuristic substring matching.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.11.6","title":"Repo hygiene: ignore generated dirs (node_modules, coverage, playwright)","description":"# Goal
Prevent accidental commits and tooling noise from generated directories.

# Background
- This repo currently has a minimal `.gitignore` and generated directories commonly appear in working trees (`node_modules`, Playwright reports, coverage, Next build output, etc.).
- This increases risk of accidentally committing artifacts and also makes tools like `ubs` noisy when scanning the repo.

# Current risk
`git ls-files` shows at least one tracked file under `node_modules/` (likely a generated Vitest cache file).

Per AGENTS.md, removing it from git requires a destructive git operation; I need explicit user-provided approval before I can run or propose the exact command.

# Scope
- Expand `.gitignore` with common generated directories and files:
  - `node_modules/`
  - `apps/web/node_modules/`
  - `apps/web/.next/`
  - `apps/web/playwright-report/`
  - `apps/web/test-results/`
  - `apps/web/coverage/`
  - `dist/`
  - `*.tsbuildinfo`

# Acceptance criteria
- `.gitignore` updates prevent new generated artifacts from being staged by default.
- Follow-up is documented for cleaning the already-tracked `node_modules/` artifact (requires explicit approval).

 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.12","title":"EPIC: Science Mode v0 (Deterministic roles + experiment loop)","description":"# Goal
Make BrennerBot **runnable as a real multi-agent research lab** on a subject we choose *today*, not just as a method corpus + nice artifacts.

"Science Mode" here means:
- agents collaborate with **role separation** (hypotheses / tests / critique)
- sessions can be bound to a **target repo / target system** where experiments are runnable
- we can safely **run experiments**, capture results, and feed them back into the session as structured deltas

This epic is the missing infrastructure between:
- the **protocol kernel** (artifact schema + delta format + linter)
- and **closed-loop research** (propose → test → update)

---

# Background / Why now
We already have:
- Agent Mail threads + durable coordination
- Kickoff + DELTA + COMPILED artifact loop
- Deterministic artifact merge + lint
- Web app for browsing + lab pages

What we *still* lack for real research:
1) **Deterministic role assignment** for real Agent Mail agent identities (adjective+noun names), without relying on substring heuristics.
2) **Target binding** so a session knows what codebase/dataset/system it is talking about.
3) **Safe execution** for agent-proposed experiments (resource limits, no-network default, read-only target).
4) **Results encoding** so stdout/stderr/metrics become mergeable DELTA blocks, not prose.

Without these, the lab drifts into "protocol theater": smart conversation with no controlled contact with reality.

---

# Scope (v0)
## In scope
- Agent roster + role mapping (CLI + web)
- Target repo binding (path, optional git metadata)
- Experiment sandbox runner (fail-closed if sandbox unavailable)
- Experiment results → DELTA encoding
- Minimal happy-path UX: one operator can run a closed loop without bespoke glue

## Explicit non-goals (v0)
- A general-purpose paper/PDF ingestion system (tracked separately)
- Fully autonomous agent execution (humans-in-the-loop via ntm remains the model)
- Building a full workflow engine or queue system

---

# Deliverables / Definition of done
- A session can declare `thread_id` + `target_repo` + `agent roster`.
- Kickoffs are role-specific **based on explicit roster mapping**.
- An experiment can be run safely (sandboxed or fail-closed) and captured as a structured result.
- Results can be encoded into DELTA blocks and posted back into the Agent Mail thread.
- The compiled artifact can incorporate experiment results deterministically.

---

# Design principles (future self reminders)
- Fail closed on safety: if sandbox guarantees aren't met, require manual execution.
- Keep file sprawl minimal: store only what is needed to reproduce results.
- Treat "results" as evidence, and update artifacts in a way that preserves provenance.
- Don't overfit to one target repo; design should generalize to "codebase + runnable commands".

 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.12.1","title":"Feature: Agent roster + deterministic role assignment (CLI + web)","description":"# Goal
Make **role-separated multi-agent sessions** work with real Agent Mail identities (adjective+noun names) *deterministically*.

Today, role assignment is heuristic (string matching on recipient name). That fails for real identities like `BlueLake` / `PurpleMountain`.

This feature introduces an explicit roster mapping so that:
- the operator selects or configures which agent is "GPT/Hypotheses", "Opus/Tests", "Gemini/Critique"
- kickoffs sent via CLI/web contain the correct role block for each recipient
- the roster becomes part of session metadata (auditable + reproducible)

---

# Why this matters
The whole Brenner Loop relies on **division of labor**:
- one agent generates hypotheses
- one designs discriminative tests + potency checks
- one attacks framing + scale checks

If everyone receives the same prompt or an incorrect role prompt, we lose the multi-model advantage and converge to consensus mush.

---

# Requirements
- Roster config supports:
  - mapping `agent_mail_name -> role`
  - optional metadata: program (`codex-cli`, `claude-code`, `gemini-cli`), model string, notes
  - presets ("3-agent default")
- CLI supports:
  - `session start` with explicit per-recipient role mapping (flags or config)
  - validates roster completeness (must cover all recipients)
  - emits the roster mapping into kickoff message body (for audit)
- Web supports:
  - selecting recipients from Agent Mail agent directory
  - assigning roles in UI (dropdown per recipient)
  - using the same underlying kickoff composition logic as the CLI

---

# UX considerations
- Keep the happy path fast:
  - a "Default 3-agent roster" button that assigns roles automatically
  - a way to save/load roster presets (config)
- Fail loudly and early:
  - if roster mapping is missing for any recipient, block the send with a clear message

---

# Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Role assignment never depends on substring heuristics in production paths
- [ ] CLI can start a role-separated session using explicit roster mapping
- [ ] Web can start a role-separated session using explicit roster mapping
- [ ] Kickoff messages include an explicit "Role" section and roster provenance
- [ ] Unit tests cover role mapping edge cases

-{"id":"brenner_bot-5so.12.1.1","title":"Task: Define agent roster schema + config contract","description":"# Goal
Define a canonical, versioned representation of an **agent roster** and how it is loaded (flags/env/config/defaults).

---

# Design constraints
- Must work for both:
  - `brenner` CLI
  - `apps/web` lab mode
- Must reference **Agent Mail identity names** (e.g., `BlueLake`), not tool names.
- Must be auditable: roster should be serializable into kickoff message bodies and/or session metadata.

---

# Proposed schema (draft)
```ts
type AgentRole = 'hypothesis_generator' | 'test_designer' | 'adversarial_critic';

type AgentRosterEntry = {
  agentName: string;          // Agent Mail identity name
  role: AgentRole;
  program?: 'codex-cli' | 'claude-code' | 'gemini-cli' | string;
  model?: string;             // human-readable
  notes?: string;
};

type AgentRosterPreset = {
  name: string;               // e.g. 'default-3-agent'
  entries: AgentRosterEntry[];
};

type BrennerConfig = {
  // ...existing...
  roster?: {
    presets?: AgentRosterPreset[];
    defaultPreset?: string;
  };
};
```

---

# Loading / precedence
- CLI flags override config.
- Web uses env defaults + server-side config, but still allows per-session overrides.

---

# Edge cases to decide now
- If multiple recipients share a role: allowed? (Probably yes for "more brains" mode, but requires role prompt semantics.)
- If a recipient role is omitted: error (preferred) vs fallback to DEFAULT_ROLE.
- How to represent "unified" mode alongside roster mode.

---

# Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Schema written down as a repo contract (spec + types)
- [ ] Precedence rules documented
- [ ] Clear stance on edge cases (duplicate roles, missing mapping)

-{"id":"brenner_bot-5so.12.1.2","title":"Task: Session kickoff accepts explicit per-recipient roles","description":"# Goal
Update kickoff composition so role assignment is **explicit** and not inferred from recipient name.

---

# Current problem
`getAgentRole(agentName: string)` uses substring heuristics (`includes('codex')`, etc). Real Agent Mail identities are adjective+noun and won't match.

---

# Proposed API change (example)
Option A (minimal): extend KickoffConfig
```ts
export interface KickoffConfig {
  // existing fields...
  recipients: string[];
  recipientRoles?: Record<string, AgentRole>; // maps recipient -> role
}
```
Option B (cleaner): accept structured recipients
```ts
export interface KickoffRecipient {
  name: string;
  role: AgentRole;
}
export interface KickoffConfig {
  recipients: KickoffRecipient[];
}
```

---

# Considerations
- Must remain deterministic.
- Must keep a reasonable fallback for legacy callers (if any).
- Kickoff body should include the role assignment explicitly so recipients don't rely on memory.

---

# Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Role mapping can be provided explicitly for all recipients
- [ ] No substring heuristics in the "explicit roster" path
- [ ] Unit tests: BlueLake-style names still get correct roles

-{"id":"brenner_bot-5so.12.1.3","title":"Task: CLI session start supports roster mapping (flags + config)","description":"# Goal
Make `./brenner.ts session start` able to send role-specific kickoffs using an explicit roster mapping.

---

# Why this matters
The CLI is the canonical operator path for "real sessions" (especially local + ntm).
If roster mapping isn't supported here, operators will keep manually copy/pasting prompts.

---

# Proposed CLI UX
Pick one and document it:

## Option A: Per-recipient role map flag
```bash
./brenner.ts session start \
  --thread-id RS-20260101-foo \
  --to BlueLake,PurpleMountain,RedForest \
  --role-map "BlueLake=hypothesis_generator,PurpleMountain=test_designer,RedForest=adversarial_critic" \
  --excerpt-file excerpt.md \
  --question "..."
```

## Option B: Named roster preset (preferred for repeated usage)
```bash
./brenner.ts session start \
  --thread-id RS-20260101-foo \
  --to BlueLake,PurpleMountain,RedForest \
  --roster-preset default-3-agent \
  ...
```

---

# Behavior
- Validate that every recipient has a role mapping (unless --unified)
- Print the resolved roster mapping (json mode includes it)
- Include roster mapping in kickoff body

---

# Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Roster mapping supported in CLI (at least one of the options above)
- [ ] Validation errors are human-readable
- [ ] Unit tests cover role map parsing and error cases

-{"id":"brenner_bot-5so.12.1.4","title":"Task: Web lab session form supports recipient role assignment","description":"# Goal
Enable role-separated session kickoff from the web lab UI (`/sessions/new`) using explicit recipient→role mapping.

---

# Current state
- `/sessions/new` collects a comma-separated recipient list.
- The kickoff send route (`/api/sessions`) sends a unified prompt.
- Operator selection exists, but that's **Brenner operators**, not **agent roles**.

---

# Desired UX
- Recipients are selected from Agent Mail agent directory (with fallback to manual entry).
- For each selected recipient, the operator assigns a role:
  - Hypothesis Generator
  - Test Designer
  - Adversarial Critic
- Provide a "Default 3-agent" quick assign button.

---

# Implementation notes
- Must remain fail-closed behind lab mode + auth.
- Should reuse the same kickoff composition logic as the CLI/session-kickoff lib.

---

# Acceptance criteria
- [ ] UI supports per-recipient role assignment
- [ ] Kickoff API accepts the roster mapping and sends role-specific messages
- [ ] Error messages point to missing role assignment or invalid recipients
- [ ] E2E test covers role-assigned kickoff submission

-{"id":"brenner_bot-5so.12.1.5","title":"Task: Doctor/preflight checks for external agent CLIs + roster sanity","description":"# Goal
Reduce operator setup friction by making `brenner doctor` detect common "can't actually run a session" failures.

---

# Checks to add (presence-only; do not try to log in)
- `claude` / `claude-code` presence
- `codex` / `codex-cli` presence
- `gemini` / `gemini-cli` presence

(Exact binary names vary by install; decide on a small allowlist and document it.)

---

# Roster sanity checks
If a roster preset is configured:
- validate that each role exists at least once in the preset
- validate no unknown roles
- (optional) validate listed agent names exist in Agent Mail directory (best-effort)

---

# Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `brenner doctor` reports missing external agent CLIs clearly
- [ ] `brenner doctor --json` includes these checks
- [ ] Roster sanity warnings/errors are actionable

 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.12.2","title":"Feature: Experiment runner CLI (run → encode → post to thread)","description":"# Goal
Provide a **single operator workflow** to run a discriminative test (T#) against the bound target repo and feed the result back into the session as DELTAs.

---

# Why this matters
A Brenner Loop without a results channel stays at the level of speculation.
We need a repeatable way to:
1) run a test
2) capture stdout/stderr/metrics
3) encode it to DELTAs
4) post it back into the Agent Mail thread

---

# Proposed operator workflow
```bash
# 1) Run experiment in sandbox
./brenner.ts experiment run --thread-id RS-... --test-id T1 --command "..."

# 2) Encode results to delta message
./brenner.ts experiment encode --thread-id RS-... --test-id T1 --result-file results.json > deltas.md

# 3) Post DELTA message to thread
./brenner.ts mail send --thread-id RS-... --subject "DELTA[results]: T1 run" --body-file deltas.md --to <operator/agents>

# 4) Compile artifact
./brenner.ts session compile --thread-id RS-...
```

Longer term: a single `experiment run --post` command can do (1)-(3) automatically.

---

# Design notes
- Keep it composable: `experiment run` produces a structured JSON result file that can be audited.
- Treat posting results as a deliberate action (avoid silent side effects).
- Preserve provenance: command, timestamp, target repo binding, git SHA if available.

---

# Acceptance criteria
- [ ] CLI supports `experiment run` using SessionBinding + sandbox
- [ ] CLI can produce an Agent Mail-ready delta message body from results
- [ ] Operator can post results back into the thread and compile an updated artifact

+{"id":"brenner_bot-5so.12.4","title":"Feature: One-command cockpit start (ntm + roster + kickoff)","description":"# Goal
Reduce the operator overhead of starting a multi-agent session.

---

# Problem
Today, operators do many manual steps:
- pick thread id
- spawn ntm session
- compose excerpt
- send kickoff
- tell agents to check mail

This is doable, but high-friction.

---

# Desired UX
A single command should get us to "agents are working" with minimal copy/paste:

```bash
brenner cockpit start \
  --thread-id RS-... \
  --roster-preset default-3-agent \
  --excerpt-file excerpt.md \
  --question "..."
```

It may:
- spawn ntm session (optional flag)
- send kickoff messages (role-separated)
- broadcast "check mail" to all panes
- print next commands (status/compile)

---

# Constraints
- Remain humans-in-the-loop (no attempt to drive external CLIs programmatically).
- Avoid hidden side effects; provide `--dry-run` option.

---

# Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `brenner cockpit start` exists (or equivalent)
- [ ] Works with roster mapping (deterministic roles)
- [ ] Has a dry-run mode that prints commands without executing

 {"id":"brenner_bot-5so.13","title":"EPIC: Topic evidence packs v0 (papers/notes/datasets with stable anchors)","description":"# Goal
Enable BrennerBot sessions to operate on **arbitrary research subjects** (not just codebases) by supporting a lightweight "topic evidence pack" with stable anchors and citations.

---

# Why this matters
Right now, artifact provenance conventions are Brenner-transcript-specific (`§n`).
To do real research on a chosen subject, we need a way to:
- collect the minimal evidence we are using (papers, notes, dataset descriptions)
- cite it in artifacts with stable anchors
- avoid drifting into model-memory speculation

---

# Design stance (v0)
- Keep it *excerpt-first*: store only the evidence excerpts we actually use.
- Keep it *anchored*: every excerpt gets a stable ID (e.g., `S1.P3`, `S2#p5`).
- Keep it *local-first*: topic packs are for lab mode and local workflows, not public site.

---

# Non-goals (v0)
- A full bibliographic manager
- Crawling the web automatically
- Shipping copyrighted PDFs to production

---

# Definition of done
- A session can optionally attach a topic evidence pack.
- Artifacts can cite evidence pack anchors in addition to Brenner `§n` anchors.
- Search/browsing of evidence pack is possible in lab mode.

 {"id":"brenner_bot-ysc","title":"Task: Implement swipe navigation between documents","description":"Add gesture-based navigation between documents on mobile.

## Purpose

Swipe navigation is a natural mobile pattern. Users expect to swipe left/right to go between items.

## Scope

- Swipe LEFT to go to NEXT document
- Swipe RIGHT to go to PREVIOUS document
- Only on document pages (/corpus/[doc])
- Threshold: 50% of screen width or 100px, whichever is less

## Technical Approach

### Option A: CSS Scroll Snap
- Wrap prev/current/next in scroll container
- Use scroll-snap-type for snapping
- Detect scroll end for navigation

### Option B: Touch Event Handling
- Listen for touchstart/touchmove/touchend
- Calculate swipe distance and velocity
- Trigger navigation if threshold met

### Option C: Library (react-swipeable)
- Use existing gesture library
- Less code, more reliable
- Small bundle impact (~3KB)

Recommended: Option C for reliability, unless bundle size is critical.

## Visual Feedback

- Show edge peek of prev/next during swipe
- Elastic bounce at first/last document
- Cancel swipe if released before threshold

## Accessibility

- Swipe is enhancement, not requirement
- Prev/Next buttons always visible
- Works without gesture support

## Files to Modify

- Create gesture wrapper component
- Wrap document page content
- Handle navigation events

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Swipe left/right navigates documents
- [ ] Visual feedback during swipe
- [ ] Threshold prevents accidental navigation
- [ ] Edge behavior at first/last doc
- [ ] Doesn't interfere with content scrolling
- [ ] Prev/Next buttons still work","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:31:13.330351376-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T07:21:30.339121607-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T07:21:30.339121607-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented swipe navigation on /corpus/[doc]","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-ysc","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-0dk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:31:24.946600519-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

```markdown

## Setup

3. Note your assigned role (if role-specific session)

## Your Role
Check the thread for role assignment. Common roles:
- **Hypothesis Generator**: Propose hypotheses including third alternative

Reply with structured DELTA blocks per specs/delta_output_format_v0.1.md

3. Ground claims in transcript anchors (§n) or label [inference]

## What to Contribute

- Hypotheses (including third alternative)
- Predictions for each hypothesis
- Discriminative tests with potency checks
- Assumptions with load/test analysis
- Critiques of the framing

## Check Before Submitting
- [ ] Each delta has operation, section, payload, rationale
- [ ] Claims are grounded or labeled [inference]
- [ ] Response addresses gaps in current artifact
```

## 3. brenner_round_complete.md
```markdown
# Round Complete: {{session}}

## What Happens Next

2. Artifact is updated with all DELTAs
3. COMPILED message posted to thread

- Review any pending ack_required messages

 {"id":"brenner_bot-zos","title":"TanStack Table: Upgrade CrosswalkTable with Sorting","description":"# Goal
Upgrade the CrosswalkTable component to use TanStack Table for proper sorting, filtering, and accessibility.

## Background
CrosswalkTable shows a comparison matrix:
- Rows: 5 concepts (Problem Selection, Hypothesis Generation, etc.)
- Columns: 3 models (Opus, GPT, Gemini)
- Cells: Each model's interpretation of the concept

Current implementation is a simple map with hover states:
```typescript
const CROSSWALK_DATA: CrosswalkRow[] = [
  { concept: '...', opus: '...', gpt: '...', gemini: '...' },
  // ... 5 rows
];

{CROSSWALK_DATA.map((row, i) => (
  <tr onMouseEnter={() => setHoveredRow(i)}>
    <td>{row.concept}</td>
    <td>{row.opus}</td>
    <td>{row.gpt}</td>
    <td>{row.gemini}</td>
  </tr>
))}
```

## Why TanStack Table?
Even for a small table, Table provides:
1. **Sorting**: Click column headers to sort
2. **Column visibility**: Toggle which models to show
3. **Accessibility**: Proper ARIA attributes, keyboard navigation
4. **Future-proof**: Easy to add filtering, pagination later

## Implementation

### 1. Define columns
```typescript
const columns = useMemo(() => [
  columnHelper.accessor('concept', {
    header: 'Concept',
    cell: info => <span className="font-medium">{info.getValue()}</span>,
  }),
  columnHelper.accessor('opus', {
    header: () => <ModelBadge model="opus" />,
    cell: info => info.getValue(),
  }),
  columnHelper.accessor('gpt', {
    header: () => <ModelBadge model="gpt" />,
    cell: info => info.getValue(),
  }),
  columnHelper.accessor('gemini', {
    header: () => <ModelBadge model="gemini" />,
    cell: info => info.getValue(),
  }),
], []);
```

### 2. Create table instance
```typescript
const table = useReactTable({
  data: CROSSWALK_DATA,
  columns,
  getCoreRowModel: getCoreRowModel(),
  getSortedRowModel: getSortedRowModel(),
  state: { sorting },
  onSortingChange: setSorting,
});
```

### 3. Render with Table primitives
```typescript
<table>
  <thead>
    {table.getHeaderGroups().map(headerGroup => (
      <tr key={headerGroup.id}>
        {headerGroup.headers.map(header => (
          <th 
            key={header.id}
            onClick={header.column.getToggleSortingHandler()}
            className="cursor-pointer"
          >
            {flexRender(header.column.columnDef.header, header.getContext())}
            {{
              asc: ' ↑',
              desc: ' ↓',
            }[header.column.getIsSorted() as string] ?? null}
          </th>
        ))}
      </tr>
    ))}
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    {table.getRowModel().rows.map(row => (
      <tr key={row.id}>
        {row.getVisibleCells().map(cell => (
          <td key={cell.id}>
            {flexRender(cell.column.columnDef.cell, cell.getContext())}
          </td>
        ))}
      </tr>
    ))}
  </tbody>
</table>
```

## Enhancements Over Current

### Sorting
Click any column header to sort:
- Alphabetical by concept
- Alphabetical by model interpretation

### Column Visibility (Optional)
Add toggles to show/hide specific models.
Useful for focused comparison.

### Cell Click to Navigate
Make cells clickable to navigate to that model's full distillation.

### Keyboard Navigation
- Arrow keys to move between cells
- Enter to activate cell link
- Tab for focus management

## Considerations

### Styling
Keep the existing model-specific color coding.
Table primitives are unstyled - apply existing Tailwind classes.

### Mobile
On mobile, table may need horizontal scroll.
Consider: Card view on mobile, table on desktop.

### Row Hover
Maintain existing row hover highlight.
Table provides row.getIsSelected() etc. for states.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Table uses TanStack Table
- [ ] Column sorting works
- [ ] Keyboard navigation works
- [ ] Existing styling preserved
- [ ] Model badges in headers
- [ ] Cell links to distillations work

+# Brenner Protocol: Agent Roster Schema v0.1
+
+> **Status**: Draft specification
+> **Purpose**: Define canonical representation of agent-to-role mappings for multi-agent sessions
+> **Depends on**: `role_prompts_v0.1.md`, `agent_mail_contracts_v0.1.md`
+
+---
+
+## Overview
+
+An **agent roster** maps Agent Mail identity names (e.g., `BlueLake`, `PurpleMountain`) to Brenner Protocol roles. This explicit mapping replaces the heuristic string-matching approach (checking if names contain "Codex", "Claude", etc.) that fails for real Agent Mail identities.
+
+### Why This Matters
+
+The Brenner Loop relies on **division of labor**:
+- Hypothesis Generator: hunts paradoxes, imports cross-domain patterns
+- Test Designer: designs discriminative tests with potency controls
+- Adversarial Critic: attacks framing, checks scale constraints
+
+Without explicit role assignment, all agents receive the same prompt or incorrect role prompts, losing the multi-model advantage.
+
+---
+
+## Core Types
+
+### AgentRole
+
+The three canonical roles in the Brenner Protocol:
+
+```typescript
+type AgentRole =
+  | "hypothesis_generator"  // Primary: Codex/GPT
+  | "test_designer"         // Primary: Opus/Claude
+  | "adversarial_critic";   // Primary: Gemini
+```
+
+### RosterEntry
+
+A single agent-to-role mapping:
+
+```typescript
+interface RosterEntry {
+  /** Agent Mail identity name (e.g., "BlueLake"). REQUIRED. */
+  agentName: string;
+
+  /** Assigned role for this session. REQUIRED. */
+  role: AgentRole;
+
+  /** CLI tool used to run this agent. Optional. */
+  program?: "codex-cli" | "claude-code" | "gemini-cli" | string;
+
+  /** Model identifier (human-readable). Optional. */
+  model?: string;
+
+  /** Free-form notes for audit trail. Optional. */
+  notes?: string;
+}
+```
+
+### Roster
+
+A complete roster for a session:
+
+```typescript
+interface Roster {
+  /** List of agent-to-role mappings. At least one entry required. */
+  entries: RosterEntry[];
+
+  /** Mode indicator. Default: "role_separated". */
+  mode?: "role_separated" | "unified";
+
+  /** Human-readable name for this roster. Optional. */
+  name?: string;
+
+  /** When this roster was created/last modified. Optional. */
+  createdAt?: string;  // ISO 8601
+}
+```
+
+### RosterPreset
+
+A saved roster configuration for reuse:
+
+```typescript
+interface RosterPreset {
+  /** Unique identifier for this preset. REQUIRED. */
+  id: string;
+
+  /** Display name. REQUIRED. */
+  name: string;
+
+  /** Description of when to use this preset. Optional. */
+  description?: string;
+
+  /** The roster entries. REQUIRED. */
+  entries: Omit<RosterEntry, "agentName">[];
+
+  /** Placeholder names that get replaced at runtime. Optional. */
+  placeholderNames?: string[];
+}
+```
+
+---
+
+## Roster Modes
+
+### Role-Separated Mode (Default)
+
+Each recipient gets a role-specific prompt. This is the standard Brenner Protocol approach.
+
+```typescript
+const roster: Roster = {
+  mode: "role_separated",
+  entries: [
+    { agentName: "BlueLake", role: "hypothesis_generator", program: "codex-cli" },
+    { agentName: "PurpleMountain", role: "test_designer", program: "claude-code" },
+    { agentName: "GreenValley", role: "adversarial_critic", program: "gemini-cli" },
+  ],
+};
+```
+
+### Unified Mode
+
+All recipients get the same prompt (no role differentiation). Useful for simple sessions or when using a single powerful model.
+
+```typescript
+const roster: Roster = {
+  mode: "unified",
+  entries: [
+    { agentName: "BlueLake", role: "hypothesis_generator" },  // Role ignored in unified mode
+    { agentName: "PurpleMountain", role: "hypothesis_generator" },
+  ],
+};
+```
+
+---
+
+## Edge Cases and Rules
+
+### Rule 1: No Duplicate Agents
+
+Each `agentName` may appear at most once in a roster. Attempting to add the same agent twice is an error.
+
+```typescript
+// ERROR: BlueLake appears twice
+const invalid: Roster = {
+  entries: [
+    { agentName: "BlueLake", role: "hypothesis_generator" },
+    { agentName: "BlueLake", role: "test_designer" },  // INVALID
+  ],
+};
+```
+
+### Rule 2: Duplicate Roles ARE Allowed
+
+Multiple agents may share the same role (for "more brains" mode):
+
+```typescript
+// VALID: Two hypothesis generators
+const valid: Roster = {
+  entries: [
+    { agentName: "BlueLake", role: "hypothesis_generator" },
+    { agentName: "RedSky", role: "hypothesis_generator" },  // OK
+    { agentName: "PurpleMountain", role: "test_designer" },
+  ],
+};
+```
+
+### Rule 3: Missing Mapping = Error
+
+If a recipient is in the `recipients` list but not in the roster, the kickoff MUST fail with a clear error. No silent fallback to DEFAULT_ROLE.
+
+```typescript
+// ERROR: "GreenValley" in recipients but not in roster
+composeKickoffMessages({
+  recipients: ["BlueLake", "PurpleMountain", "GreenValley"],  // 3 recipients
+  roster: {
+    entries: [
+      { agentName: "BlueLake", role: "hypothesis_generator" },
+      { agentName: "PurpleMountain", role: "test_designer" },
+      // GreenValley missing!
+    ],
+  },
+  // ...
+});
+// → Error: "Missing roster entry for recipient: GreenValley"
+```
+
+### Rule 4: Extra Roster Entries Are Allowed
+
+The roster may contain entries for agents not in the recipients list. This allows presets to be reused.
+
+```typescript
+// VALID: Roster has extra entries
+composeKickoffMessages({
+  recipients: ["BlueLake"],  // 1 recipient
+  roster: {
+    entries: [
+      { agentName: "BlueLake", role: "hypothesis_generator" },
+      { agentName: "PurpleMountain", role: "test_designer" },  // Extra, ignored
+    ],
+  },
+});
+// → OK: Only BlueLake gets a message
+```
+
+### Rule 5: Role Validation
+
+The `role` field must be one of the three canonical roles. Unknown roles are errors.
+
+```typescript
+// ERROR: Invalid role
+const invalid: RosterEntry = {
+  agentName: "BlueLake",
+  role: "researcher",  // INVALID - not a canonical role
+};
+```
+
+---
+
+## Loading Precedence
+
+Roster configuration can come from multiple sources. The precedence order (highest to lowest):
+
+1. **CLI flags** (`--roster`, `--agent-role`)
+2. **Environment variables** (`BRENNER_ROSTER_*`)
+3. **Session-specific config** (web form, API payload)
+4. **Project config** (`brenner.config.ts` or `.brennerrc`)
+5. **Preset lookup** (if `--preset` flag used)
+
+### CLI Flag Examples
+
+```bash
+# Explicit per-agent mapping
+brenner session start \
+  --to BlueLake --role hypothesis_generator \
+  --to PurpleMountain --role test_designer \
+  --to GreenValley --role adversarial_critic
+
+# Using a preset
+brenner session start --preset default-3-agent \
+  --to BlueLake --to PurpleMountain --to GreenValley
+
+# Inline JSON roster
+brenner session start --roster '[
+  {"agentName":"BlueLake","role":"hypothesis_generator"},
+  {"agentName":"PurpleMountain","role":"test_designer"}
+]'
+```
+
+### Config File Example
+
+```typescript
+// brenner.config.ts
+export default {
+  roster: {
+    presets: [
+      {
+        id: "default-3-agent",
+        name: "Default 3-Agent Setup",
+        description: "Standard Brenner Protocol with Codex/Opus/Gemini",
+        entries: [
+          { role: "hypothesis_generator", program: "codex-cli", model: "GPT-5.2" },
+          { role: "test_designer", program: "claude-code", model: "Opus 4.5" },
+          { role: "adversarial_critic", program: "gemini-cli", model: "Gemini 3" },
+        ],
+      },
+    ],
+    defaultPreset: "default-3-agent",
+  },
+};
+```
+
+---
+
+## Serialization in Kickoff Messages
+
+The roster MUST be serialized into the kickoff message body for audit purposes. This ensures reproducibility and allows future sessions to understand the role assignment.
+
+### Format in Message Body
+
+```markdown
+## Session Configuration
+
+**Roster Mode**: role_separated
+**Roster Name**: Default 3-Agent Setup
+
+| Agent | Role | Program | Model |
+|-------|------|---------|-------|
+| BlueLake | hypothesis_generator | codex-cli | GPT-5.2 |
+| PurpleMountain | test_designer | claude-code | Opus 4.5 |
+| GreenValley | adversarial_critic | gemini-cli | Gemini 3 |
+
+---
+```
+
+### JSON in Artifact Metadata
+
+```json
+{
+  "metadata": {
+    "session_id": "RS-20251230-cell-fate",
+    "roster": {
+      "mode": "role_separated",
+      "name": "Default 3-Agent Setup",
+      "entries": [
+        { "agentName": "BlueLake", "role": "hypothesis_generator", "program": "codex-cli" },
+        { "agentName": "PurpleMountain", "role": "test_designer", "program": "claude-code" },
+        { "agentName": "GreenValley", "role": "adversarial_critic", "program": "gemini-cli" }
+      ]
+    }
+  }
+}
+```

+
+## Validation Functions
+
+### validateRoster
+
+```typescript
+function validateRoster(roster: Roster): ValidationResult {
+  const errors: string[] = [];
+  const seenAgents = new Set<string>();
+
+  for (const entry of roster.entries) {
+    // Check for duplicate agents
+    if (seenAgents.has(entry.agentName)) {
+      errors.push(`Duplicate agent in roster: ${entry.agentName}`);
+    }
+    seenAgents.add(entry.agentName);
+
+    // Check role validity
+    const validRoles = ["hypothesis_generator", "test_designer", "adversarial_critic"];
+    if (!validRoles.includes(entry.role)) {
+      errors.push(`Invalid role for ${entry.agentName}: ${entry.role}`);
+    }
+  }
+
+  return { valid: errors.length === 0, errors };
+}
+```
+
+### validateRosterCoverage
+
+```typescript
+function validateRosterCoverage(
+  roster: Roster,
+  recipients: string[]
+): ValidationResult {
+  const errors: string[] = [];
+  const rosterAgents = new Set(roster.entries.map(e => e.agentName));
+
+  for (const recipient of recipients) {
+    if (!rosterAgents.has(recipient)) {
+      errors.push(`Missing roster entry for recipient: ${recipient}`);
+    }
+  }
+
+  return { valid: errors.length === 0, errors };
+}
+```

+
+## Backwards Compatibility
+
+### Heuristic Fallback (Deprecated)
+
+The existing `getAgentRole()` function that matches on substrings ("Codex" → hypothesis_generator) will be **deprecated** but remain available as a fallback for:
+- Legacy sessions without explicit rosters
+- Quick testing where roster setup is overhead
+
+To use the fallback, set `roster.mode = "heuristic"` (not recommended for production):
+
+```typescript
+const legacyRoster: Roster = {
+  mode: "heuristic" as any,  // DEPRECATED
+  entries: [],  // Empty - uses substring matching
+};
+```
+
+### Migration Path
+
+1. **v0.1** (now): Add roster schema, make it optional, warn if not provided
+2. **v0.2** (future): Make roster required for role_separated mode
+3. **v1.0** (future): Remove heuristic fallback entirely
